name: Pappardelle with Duck Ragù

ingredients.item: free-range duck breasts, trimmed of excess fat and sliced
red wine, such as pinot noir
sea salt and ground black pepper

method: Heat the butter and oil in a saucepan and brown the duck pieces all over. Set aside on a plate.

To the same pan add the pancetta, onion, garlic, carrot, celery and herbs and cook for a few minutes, until they begin to colour. Add the duck pieces, with any juices that have pooled on the plate, and the wine. Stir well, scraping off anything sticking to the bottom of the pan. When the wine has evaporated, add the tomato paste. Cook for a few minutes, then stir in the tomatoes.
Put a lid on the pan and cook over low heat for about 45 minutes, stirring from time to time. The sauce should become thick and rich and the duck meat very tender. Taste for salt and pepper.

Bring a large pan of salted water to the boil. Cook the pappardelle until aldente. Drain the pasta and put it into a warm serving dish. Add the ragù and combine well. Serve immediately with parmigiano.
